---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: true
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]

---

```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{Clinical Research Methodology\\ \vspace{1em} \emph{B. Activity}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{../Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{../Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{\today}
\maketitle
```

##
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
{\Large \textbf{How to choose and evaluate a test? \\(summary from previous lesson)}}

\end{center}
```


## A hypothetical test (test A)

 Task  | Raw Score | Percentile | Cut-off |
|--------------------------|------------------|------------|---------|
| Memory                  | 45               | 60         |         |
| Attention               | 30               | 25         | *       |
| Language               | 50               | 75         |         |
| Executive Functions       | 28               | 15         | *       |
| Visuospatial            | 40               | 50         |         |
| Processing Speed | 22               | 10         | *       |

## A hypothetical test (test B)

 Task  | Raw Score | Percentile |  |
|--------------------------|------------------|------------|---------|
| Memory                  | 45               | 60         |         |
| Attention               | 30               | 25         |         |
| Language               | 50               | 75         |         |
| Executive Functions       | 28               | 15         |         |
| Visuospatial            | 40               | 50         |         |
| Processing Speed | 22               | 10         |         |

## Wooclap

![](../Figures/wooclap_metodi.png){fig-align="center"}

##
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
{\Large \textbf{What does it tell us when a performance is below the cut-off? And why is it useful?}}

\includegraphics[width=0.3\linewidth]{../Figures/Padlet_properties_qr_code.png}
\end{center}
```

##

Construction of thresholds

##
```{=latex}
{\Large \textbf{How are the thresholds used in clinical practice constructed?}}
```


##
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
{\Large \textbf{When might comparison with norms not be adequate?}}

\includegraphics[width=0.3\linewidth]{../Figures/Padlet_properties_qr_code.png}
\end{center}
```


##
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
{\Large \textbf{What happens to Specificity if Sensitivity increases?}}

\includegraphics[width=0.3\linewidth]{../Figures/Padlet_properties_qr_code.png}
\end{center}
```
